rulings.Case Title: INFAC MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, VS. COMDISCO INVESTMENT GROUP, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Deposition Subpoena And Application For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 18, 2011, Line 3. OTHER SAMUEL HALLE II'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena And Application For Protective Order. Denied. Defendant¿s request for sanctions is denied. Moving party¿s asserted right to indemnification is not a basis to deny otherwise valid discovery. While the moving party is not required to divulge information protected by the attorney-client and mediation privileges, at this juncture it cannot be determined what is discoverable and what is privileged. Moving party must appear for deposition no later than December 16, 2011. The motion did not lack substantial justification. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID NEUBAUER VS. UNUM GROUP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Independent Medical Exmination Of Pltf
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 18, 2011, Line 5, DEFENDANT ROYAL MACCABEES LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY, REASSURE AMERICA LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY Notice Of Motion To Compel Independent Medical Exmination Of Plaintiff. Granted in part. Plaintiff is required to attend an IME to be conducted by the unnamed physician referred to in paragraph 15 of Mr. McLeod¿s first declaration. The IME will require plaintiff to travel approximately 67 miles each way. At plaintiff¿s option, he may choose to divide the travel over three days so that he is not required to travel more than 50 miles per day and the defendant must pay the cost of a driver to transport plaintiff to the IME as well as reasonable costs of meals and lodging. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. PLAINTIFFS ADOUBOU TRAORE, JOSEPH SCIARRILLO, BINIAM FANTAY AND LARRY SAXXON'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Their Requests For Admission, Set One; Form Interrogatories - General, Set One; And Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One, And For Sanctions. Granted. Defendant must provide further verified responses and pay sanctions of $2640 (10 hours at $260 per hour plus $40) by December 9, 2011. Discovery requests served on a non-human party such as defendant requires the party to disclose all information that is known by the party, including all of its employees and agents, not just the person designated to verify the responses. rulings.Case Title: JOANNE FUNG VS. ON LOK INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 18, 2011, Line 6, DEFENDANT ON LOK, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is denied and the alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the second cause of action for disability discrimination claim and denied as to all other claims. Plaintiff concedes that her pregnancy did not constitute a disability. There are triable issues whether: 1) plaintiff was properly performing her job, 2) there is a causal nexus between the leave request and the termination, and 3) defendant discharged plaintiff for a legitimate reason. Plaintiff¿s evidence of pretext includes the following: defendant denied plaintiff¿s formal leave request, defendant was not planning to hire a new person to replace plaintiff, Ms. Chang was charged with committing the same wrongdoing but was not discharged, and defendant agreed to mischaracterize the termination. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with CCP 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BARRY G. INGRAM VS. 24HR FITNESS CENTERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 18, 2011, Line 19, DEFENDANTs 24 HOUR FITNESS USA, INC, JIM ROWLEY, BOBBI QUICK, JUAN RAMIREZ, TYLER HANSEN DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend as to the defamation and Government Code claims and sustained with ten days eave to amend as to the discrimination and breach of contract claims. The defamation claim is barred by the one-year statute of limitations of CCP 340(c). A Government Code tort claim only applies to public entities and employees. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege facts showing discriminatory intent and how the alleged contract was breached. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
